exemplar.title: To what extent did Walmart's acquisition impact Flipkart's competitive position in India Market?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent did Walmart's acquisition impact Flipkart's competitive position in India Market?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation throughout the introduction, analysis and conclusion.
Research question is clearly stated, well focused and consistently revisited to guide the discussion.
A broad and appropriate range of relevant secondary sources and standard business tools are selected with clear evidence of informed choice.
weaknesses: Minor lapses in phrasing and occasional small digressions momentarily interrupt the narrative flow.
Source mix includes a few lower-reliability outlets (e.g. Daily Mail, blog posts).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there are very minor phrasing weaknesses and occasional digressions, overall the investigation communicates its topic and research question with precision and depth. The questionââTo what extent did Walmartâs acquisition impact Flipkartâs competitive position in the Indian market?ââis tightly focused and drives every section. The literature and methods selection is wide, relevant and clearly informed. These attributes place the work firmly in the 5â6 band, and the consistent high quality of focus and methodological rigour justifies awarding the top mark of 6.

strengths: Source material is integrated in a clearly relevant and appropriate manner to underpin key points on revenues, margins and competitive factors.
Subject knowledge of supply-chain dynamics, financial ratios and Porterâs Five Forces is coherent and effectively applied to the Flipkart case.
Business terminology (e.g. NPM, EV/Sales, ROIC) is used accurately and consistently in context.
weaknesses: A handful of statistics and benchmarks are presented without full citation or contextualisation.
Occasional source-credibility concerns (e.g. non-academic blogs) could be replaced with peer-reviewed reports.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a few missing citations and minor source-credibility issues, the essay demonstrates a clear, coherent and authoritative grasp of the subject matter. Source material is applied directly to the inquiry, and terminology is precise. These strengths show the work meets all the descriptors of the 5â6 band in Knowledge and Understanding, warranting the maximum score of 6.

strengths: Research draws on a wide array of financial data, industry forecasts and regulatory commentary that remain consistently relevant to the research question.
Analysis is logical, well supported by quantitative and qualitative evidence, and demonstrates sophisticated linkage back to competitive position.
Argument development balances advantages (e.g. supply-chain improvements) and drawbacks (e.g. profitability challenges) in a reasoned, critically aware narrative.
weaknesses: Some critical discussions (e.g. Porter model limitations, pandemic effects) could be deepened further.
The work relies entirely on secondary sources, limiting opportunities for primary-data originality.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation delivers excellent, consistently relevant research, with a high level of analytical sophistication and critically reasoned evaluation. While there is minor room to expand on methodological limitations or primary-data insight, the depth and coherence of analysis and the quality of argument place this work at the top of the 10â12 band. A full 12 marks appropriately reflect its outstanding critical thinking quality.

strengths: Structure fully adheres to the extended-essay format: clear title page, abstract, logically ordered sections, conclusion and comprehensive reference list.
Headings and subheadings guide the reader smoothly through the argument.
All required elements (title, abstract, introduction, analysis, conclusion, references) are present and correctly ordered.
weaknesses: Citation formatting is sometimes inconsistent and page numbers are missing.
A few figure captions and URLs lack full bibliographic detail.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation of the investigation exemplifies the highest band for structure; the layout and formatting choices effectively support readability and coherence. Although there are minor inconsistencies in citation style and missing page numbers, these do not undermine the overall clarity and professional arrangement of the work. Accordingly, the maximum 4 marks are justified for Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research questionââTo what extent did Walmartâs acquisition impact Flipkartâs competitive position in the Indian market?ââis explicitly stated in the abstract and introduction and is revisited in the conclusion.  It is sufficiently narrow (single acquisition, single company, single market, single dimension of performance) and strongly connected to the ensuing discussion.  Although âcompetitive positionâ could have been operationalised more tightly, the focus remains consistent throughout, justifying a high-band award.

Arguments are developed logically, weighing advantages (supply-chain, bargaining power) against drawbacks (continuing losses, Amazon rivalry).  Some evaluation is presentâe.g., recognition that growth has not translated into profitability and that data sources have limitationsâyet critiques are mostly surface-level and do not probe methodological weaknesses.  Hence the work sits in âwell-developed argument with partially critical evaluationâ.

The student demonstrates clear knowledge of core competencies, supply-chain efficiency, financial ratios and Porterâs forces, applying them coherently to Flipkart.  The narrative is generally accurate, yet explanations of ROIC decline and EV/Sales could be deeper and some assertions about regulatory loopholes lack substantiation, so the knowledge, while clear, is not fully authoritativeâappropriate for the 3-4 band.

Business terminology such as NPM, EV/Sales, ROIC, core competencies and âcompetitive rivalryâ is used accurately and in the correct context throughout.  Minor slips (e.g., âInterpriseâ value, inconsistent Rs notation) reduce consistency, placing the work at the upper end of âmostly accurateâ rather than the fault-free use expected for the top band.

The student analyses revenue trends, losses, EV/Sales and Porter factors, linking them to competitive position with reasonable supporting evidence.  Conclusions about causation (e.g., Walmartâs investment solely doubling revenue) are sometimes asserted rather than fully proved, and pandemic effects are acknowledged only briefly.  The analysis is clear and generally well-supported but lacks the sophistication for the 7â9 range.

Figures, tables and quotations are integrated to support points on revenue growth, loss reduction and bargaining power, showing mostly relevant application of source material.  A few claims (e.g., Supermart success, EV/Sales interpretation) are asserted with limited evidence, and some statistics are copied without full contextualisation, preventing a top-band judgement but satisfying âmostly relevant and appropriateâ usage.

The essay draws on a generally relevant range of secondary sourcesâcompany reports, Statista data, academic articles and news outletsâand applies standard business tools (Porter, financial ratios).  Citations are frequent, showing informed selection.  However, primary data are absent and some sources (blogs, Daily Mail) are of mixed reliability, signalling that selection was only partly âeffective and informedâ, placing the work solidly in the 3-4 band.

The research assembles financial statements, industry forecasts, supplier negotiations and regulatory commentary, all clearly relevant to assessing competitive position.  Coverage is broad and well aligned to the question, though reliance on publicly available secondary data limits originality.  Relevance is sustained across sections, justifying placement in the 7â9 band but nearer its lower edge.

Basic layout conventions (headings hierarchy, tables, figure captions) are generally respected, and a works-cited list is provided.  However, page numbers are absent in the excerpt, citation style is inconsistent, and some images are blurry or unreferenced in-text.  These shortcomings show that while layout has been considered, several requirements are mishandled, aligning with the 1â2 band.

The student states the topic in the title, re-states it in the abstract and introduction, and repeatedly links sections back to the overarching idea of Flipkartâs competitive position.  The context of Indian e-commerce, the acquisition details and why the question matters for foreign entrants are explained clearly.  Minor lapses in phrasing and the occasional digression keep the work from a perfect 6, but overall the topic is communicated accurately and with a comprehensive explanatory background.

The essay follows the prescribed EE structureâtitle page, abstract, contents (implicit), introduction, analysis sections, conclusion and full reference list.  Headings are clear and logical, facilitating reader navigation, and required elements are present.  Therefore the structure is âclearly appropriateâ for the highest band in Presentation.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated and tightly focused on Walmartâs acquisition impact on Flipkartâs competitive position, guiding the subsequent analysis.
The report lacks a word count at the beginning, which is required for IB Extended Essays compliance.
Title succinctly communicates the focus on measuring Walmartâs acquisition impact, establishing a clear and effective topic for the investigation.
Competitive rivalry section describes price wars but omits comparative market-share data; incorporate quantitative metrics for a more robust analysis.
Reliance on a Daily Mail article for foundational background introduces a source credibility issue; consider substituting with peer-reviewed or industry-standard reports.
Page numbers are absent from the header, which hinders navigation and does not meet layout conventions.
The research question is consistently reiterated in the introduction, reinforcing focus and ensuring each section remains connected to the overarching inquiry.
The core competencies section defines the concept but lacks Flipkart-specific operational data; integrate quantitative metrics to solidify application.
Analysis of supply-chain improvements relies heavily on a single Shipsy article; incorporate official reports or primary interviews to strengthen evidence.
BestPrice citations lack an in-text reference style; include author and date inline or as footnotes to meet citation consistency requirements.
Table caption formatting is inconsistent; the table title and source citation should follow a unified style guide and include complete source details.
The benchmark âexcellent retail NPM 0.5â3.5%â lacks a citation; provide the source to validate this industry standard.
The notation $$\overline{216.5B}$$ Rs is non-standard; revise to plain text or standard inline math for clarity.
The analysis omits discussion of currency conversion or purchasing-power parity when comparing revenues; adding this would deepen the evaluation.
Replace placeholder symbols $$\overline{\xi}$$ and $$\bar{\tau}$$ with the explicit loss figures to improve readability.
The EV/Sales figures are presented without citing the data source; reference the original Statista or financial report for transparency.
Porterâs model is introduced without acknowledging its limitations in the Indian e-commerce context; critically discuss model scope and constraints.
Analysis covers only two of the five forces; extend the evaluation to all five to provide a comprehensive industry assessment.
The 20% supplier discount claim from Witschi et al. is asserted without hard data; include contract excerpts or supplier figures to substantiate.
Predatory-pricing loophole claims lack regulatory references; cite specific policy documents or case studies to bolster this argument.
The conclusion does not discuss study limitations or propose future research avenues; add a dedicated limitations section for balance.
Works Cited entries show inconsistent punctuation and italics use; apply a single citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) uniformly.
References are not ordered alphabetically or by date, hindering quick lookup; reorganize the list for improved presentation.
Overuse of bare URLs within list items reduces readability; include complete author-title detail before the URL.
Some citations lack access dates, which are required for electronic sources; ensure each reference includes the date accessed.
The standalone URL citation lacks author and title information, undermining reference credibility; provide full bibliographic details.
Indentation in the reference list is inconsistent between list items; adjust formatting to ensure uniform alignment.
References are predominantly web-based; integrate peer-reviewed academic articles to strengthen theoretical and empirical grounding.
Some web citations lack access dates, reducing traceability; ensure all online sources indicate when they were accessed.
There is no reflective section discussing student decision-making or learning from setbacks; consider adding reflection to demonstrate engagement.
